docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Nicholas Burke (714-709-4400; nburke@rlattorneys.com) of Resnick & Louis, P.C. for moving party/respondent.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT JAMES RIVER INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Dimiss is GRANTED. No opposition filed. The case is dismissed with prejudice.

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. NICHOLAS P. BURKE, Esq. of Resnick & Louis, P.C. (Tel: 714-709-4400) for Respondent James River Insurance Company;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: RESPONDENT JAMES RIVER INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary; And Terminating Sanctions.

Respondent stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for Respondent authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The motion is unopposed. The motion is granted. The requested relief of monetary and terminating sanctions is granted. Moving party to submit proposed order for signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Jason Yurasek; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
